message:
perf(startup): read-only auto-import probe for list, stats, status

The startup pipeline previously took the `.beads/.write.lock` for any
command that wanted to preopen storage, even when the command was a
pure read (`list`, `stats`, `status`). On a busy repository where a
long-running write (sync, auto-flush) is in flight, that meant `br
list` would block waiting for a write lock it would almost certainly
never need.

Introduce a read-only auto-import probe path that:

- Adds `auto_import_probe()` in `src/sync/mod.rs` alongside the existing
  `auto_import_probe_refreshing_witnesses()`. The new variant computes
  the same `jsonl_newer` answer via `compute_jsonl_newer_impl` but
  deliberately skips opportunistic JSONL witness metadata refresh, so
  the storage handle can stay read-only.
- Adds `supports_auto_import_read_only_probe()` in `src/main.rs` and
  flags `Commands::List`, `Commands::Stats`, `Commands::Status` as
  probe-safe.
- Loosens `build_cli_overrides()` so `read_only_fast_open` is enabled
  for those commands by default (previously required explicit
  `--no-auto-import --no-auto-flush`).
- Reworks the Phase 1/2/3 startup logic in `main()` so:
    * `should_acquire_startup_write_lock` now takes a third arg,
      `read_only_auto_import_probe`, and skips the eager lock when set.
    * The auto-import block runs `auto_import_probe()` (read-only) when
      `read_only_fast_open` is set, and only acquires the write lock and
      reopens storage writable if the probe says an import is actually
      needed.
    * Existing writable preopen paths still acquire the lock up front
      so recovery/schema-mutating opens remain serialized.

Net effect: a fresh `br list` against a clean repository never touches
the write lock, while a `br list` against a stale repository still
performs the full witnessed import — just one extra storage reopen
later in the pipeline instead of blocking at startup.

Also updates `tests/e2e_concurrency.rs::e2e_read_command_witness_refresh_waits_for_write_lock`
to drive the test through `br search Seed --json` instead of `br list`,
since `list` is now intentionally non-blocking on the write lock.
Switches the spawn helper to `Command::cargo_bin("br").expect(...)` so
the helper returns a real `Command` rather than a `cargo_bin!` macro
expansion that no longer matches the call site.

Test expectations in `read_only_fast_open_*` and
`preopen_storage_requires_write_lock_before_open` are updated to cover
the new tri-state probe argument and the auto-enabled fast-open for
the three new commands.

files.patch:
@@ -2140,6 +2140,22 @@ pub fn auto_import_probe_refreshing_witnesses(
     Ok(probe.jsonl_newer)
 }
 
+/// Check whether auto-import needs to inspect JSONL contents without mutating metadata.
+///
+/// This variant is intended for read-only startup probes. It deliberately skips
+/// the opportunistic JSONL witness refresh that
+/// [`auto_import_probe_refreshing_witnesses`] performs, so callers can use a
+/// read-only SQLite handle and reopen writable storage only if import work is
+/// actually needed.
+///
+/// # Errors
+///
+/// Returns an error if reading JSONL metadata, stored witnesses, or hashing
+/// fails.
+pub fn auto_import_probe(storage: &SqliteStorage, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<bool> {
+    compute_jsonl_newer_impl(storage, jsonl_path).map(|probe| probe.jsonl_newer)
+}
+
 fn compute_staleness_impl(
     storage: &SqliteStorage,
     jsonl_path: &Path,
